macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopens, Boosting Stocks and Easing Inflation Pressures
items.summary: The end of Iran's blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, following a U.S. response, led to oil prices dropping 14% to $81/barrel and global stocks surging, with S&P 500 up 1.3% to a record. This alleviates global economic pressures and eases Fed's inflation fight.

items.title: IMF/World Bank Spring Meetings Address Economic Stress and Geopolitical Risks
items.summary: Global leaders at the 2026 IMF/World Bank Spring Meetings discussed tightening financial conditions, Middle East energy turmoil straining debt in vulnerable countries, and a new multi-regional financial agreement for climate shocks.

items.title: Nasdaq 100 Surges Post-Crisis Despite US-Iran Tensions
items.summary: Risk assets including Nasdaq 100 posted massive gains this week as markets overlooked lingering US-Iran issues following the Strait resolution.

items.title: IMF Warns Global Economy on Brink of Recession Amid Wars and Energy Prices
items.summary: IMF report highlights threats from wars and soaring energy prices pushing the global economy toward recession, amid ongoing geopolitical complexities.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Over $520 Million in Stablecoins Exit Ethereum in 24 Hours
items.summary: More than $520 million in stablecoins were withdrawn from the Ethereum network in the past 24 hours, potentially impacting liquidity and trading dynamics. The reasons for this large-scale outflow remain unclear.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits Two-Month High Near $78,000 Amid Easing Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin surged to a two-month peak near $78,000, driven by easing geopolitical tensions and growing investor confidence. Ethereum and major altcoins also recorded gains.

items.title: Crypto Hacks Reach Record $450 Million in Two Weeks, Bitcoin Unfazed
items.summary: Crypto hacks hit an all-time high with $450 million lost across 45 protocols in two weeks, including a $280 million exploit on Drift. Bitcoin's prediction market for prices above $60K remains at 99.9% YES, showing no short-term reaction.

items.title: Bitcoin Mining Difficulty Decreases Amid Record BTC Sales
items.summary: Bitcoin mining difficulty declined on Saturday, highlighting ongoing industry challenges amid record Bitcoin sales by miners.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Managed Agents; OpenAI and LangChain Respond with Agent Tools
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Managed Agents, a cloud-hosted agent platform, prompting LangChain to launch Deep Agents Deploy as an open alternative and OpenAI to update its Agents SDK. These moves highlight intensifying competition in AI agent development.[1]
items.source: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 Faces Backlash for Performance Downgrade
items.summary: Anthropic's new Claude Opus 4.7 model drew criticism from paying users within 24 hours, with a tokenizer using 35% more tokens, long-context retrieval dropping from 78% to 32% on MRCR benchmark, and lower scores on design tests compared to GPT-4.5.[2]
items.source: Claude Opus 4.7: The Great AI Downgrade? (Truth Revealed)

items.title: China's Agibot Unveils Four New Robots and Six AI Models at 2026 Conference
items.summary: At the 2026 AGIBOT Partners Conference in Shanghai, Agibot Robotics launched four new robot models focused on productivity, six AI models, and seven solutions, targeting 100 billion yuan revenue by 2030 after surpassing 1 billion yuan in 2025.[3]
items.source: China's Agibot unveils four new robots, targets 100 billion ... - YouTube

items.title: Allbirds Stock Surges After Implementing 'Magic AI Button'
items.summary: Shoe company Allbirds saw its stock surge following a pivot to artificial intelligence, as discussed by Barron’s Roundtable panelists.[4]

items.title: Catholic Brothers Launch Acutis AI, Faith-Driven Chatbot Based on Church Teachings
items.summary: Brothers Peter and Thomas Cooney developed Acutis AI, a chatbot grounded in 2,000 years of Catholic tradition with moral frameworks and parental controls for safe, ethical use.[7]

items.title: Nvidia Prioritizes AI Chips Over Gaming GPUs Amid Shortages
items.summary: Nvidia may skip new GeForce gaming GPUs in 2026, focusing on data center chips like Blackwell and Rubin due to 91% revenue from AI, global memory shortages, and backlash over AI rendering software.[6]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie Achieves Highest-Grossing Premiere of 2026
items.summary: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie recorded the highest-grossing premiere of 2026 so far, with financials tracking slightly behind its predecessor, The Super Mario Bros. Movie.

items.title: Thick As Thieves Immersive Sim Shifts to Single-Player and Co-Op Only
items.summary: Developer Otherside Entertainment abandons PvPvE mode in its promising immersive sim heist game Thick As Thieves to focus exclusively on single-player and co-op experiences.

items.title: First Three Resident Evil Games and Breath of Fire IV Now on Steam
items.summary: The original versions of Resident Evil 1, 2, 3, and Breath of Fire IV are now available on PC via Steam, allowing players to revisit these classics.

items.title: Game Informer Show Covers Life is Strange: Reunion, Super Meat Boy 3D, and New Balatro Roguelike
items.summary: The latest Game Informer podcast reviews Life is Strange: Reunion and Super Meat Boy 3D while highlighting a new roguelike for Balatro fans.

items.title: Twin Galaxies Daily Digest Highlights Pragmata and Weekend Game Picks
items.summary: The April 18, 2026 digest recommends Capcom's sci-fi title Pragmata and other games from Game Informer for the weekend.

items.title: Conquest Targets Invincible VS Roster with 2 DLC Fighters and Open Beta
items.summary: New details reveal Conquest joining the Invincible VS roster alongside two DLC fighters and upcoming open beta information.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Tesla Launches Robotaxi Service in Dallas and Houston
items.summary: Tesla has expanded its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston, marking a significant rollout of autonomous ride-hailing in major Texas cities.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold Sells Out Completely After Limited Release
items.summary: The Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold, a new tri-foldable smartphone, has completely sold out following its limited release and final restock.

items.title: Meta Raises Prices of Quest 3 and Quest 3S VR Headsets
items.summary: Meta is increasing prices for its Quest 3 and 3S VR headsets due to rising memory chip costs exacerbated by market conditions.

items.title: iPhone 18 Pro Colors Leaked with Eye-Catching New Option
items.summary: Leaks reveal the iPhone 18 Pro series will feature new colors, with one standout shade drawing significant attention ahead of launch.

items.title: Google Chrome Rolls Out Vertical Tabs Feature
items.summary: Google Chrome has finally introduced vertical tabs, improving browser navigation and usability for users.

items.title: Casely Recalls Wireless Power Pods Over Fire and Burn Risks
items.summary: Casely is recalling its wireless power pods due to fire and burn hazards, following previous similar issues.

items.title: $4,370 Unitree R1 Humanoid Robot Now Available on AliExpress
items.summary: Unitree's R1 humanoid robot, priced at $4,370 with aerobatic capabilities, is launching internationally via AliExpress.
